title: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
subhead: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.

title: EU gives final approval to €90-billion loan for Ukraine
Ex-PM Attal distances himself from Macron in new memoir
Boris Pistorius, the man preparing Germany for war
subhead: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who started serving as defense minister under Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

title: 'I was so exhausted, I started hitting myself. They laughed': Four Cuban asylum seekers recount their ordeal at Europe's borders
subhead: The European Court of Human Rights is expected to rule in the coming weeks on the pushbacks experienced by four Cubans at the border between Lithuania and Belarus, against the backdrop of a hybrid war with Russia.

title: Léon Marchand prepares return to French pools: 'I always told myself the Paris Games were just one step'
subhead: The 23-year-old swimmer, who won four Olympic golds in 2024, is set to compete at the French Championships in Saint-Etienne in July, followed by the European Championships in August in Saint-Denis.

title: Boumidjal, the secretive rap producer behind the hits of Damso, Jul and PLK
subhead: Crowned best composer in 2024, the masked artist is nominated for the third consecutive year at Les Flammes, the French rap and R&B music awards. Having produced more than 600 tracks, he is one of the most influential beatmakers today.

Sarkozy and the mystery of the Libyan money: A look back at a legal marathon
subhead: The appeals trial over suspicions of Libyan funding for the 2007 presidential campaign began on March 16. The case is filled with a cast of shady figures.

title: The Belgian PM's troubling ambivalence toward Russia
subhead: Bart De Wever faces criticism for his unclear stances on the level of support the European Union should provide to Ukraine. The Flemish nationalist has defended himself against accusations of playing into Moscow's hands, insisting that he only aims to push the 27 member states forward.

title: A neglected jewel, France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help
subhead: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.
